payload.description: Replace stdio transport stub with rmcp child-process transport wrapper. Include process lifecycle, cancellation, timeouts, clean shutdown, and error mapping into RociError. Add integration tests against a fixture MCP server process.

Implement MCPClient methods: initialize handshake/capability negotiation, tools/list retrieval, tools/call execution. Ensure robust protocol validation, structured error mapping, and cancellation behavior. Cover malformed payloads and timeout paths with tests.

Implement MCPToolAdapter bridging MCP-discovered tools into Roci DynamicToolProvider. Map MCP input schema to DynamicTool schema, execute tools with ToolArguments + ToolExecutionContext metadata, and preserve error/result semantics. Add e2e adapter tests.

Implement RealtimeSession::connect using OpenAI Realtime WebSocket API with auth headers, session initialization, event parsing, heartbeat/keepalive, reconnect strategy, and graceful close behavior. Add integration tests with mock WebSocket server.

Add concrete OpenAI Whisper transcription provider implementing AudioProvider. Support multipart upload, language hints, MIME/content validation, timeout/retry handling, and robust error mapping. Add unit/integration tests using wiremock.

Add concrete OpenAI text-to-speech provider implementing SpeechProvider. Support format/voice/speed options, binary audio response handling, timeout/retry behavior, and structured provider errors. Add unit/integration tests.

Implement AuthManager file persistence for credentials.json: load/save APIs, schema validation, atomic writes, secure file permissions, and corruption recovery behavior. Add tests for round-trip and failure paths.
Add comprehensive tests for ClaudeCodeAuth, GitHubCopilotAuth, and OpenAiCodexAuth including success flows, token refresh behavior, pending/slowdown/denied states, and error mapping.
Add full unit test matrix for stream transforms (filter/map/buffer/throttle): correctness, ordering, flush semantics, and error propagation.
Add tests for configuration/auth value resolution: RociConfig::from_env mapping precedence and AuthValue::resolve behavior for ApiKey/BearerToken/EnvVar with failure paths.
Add tests for error classification helpers (category, retryability, recovery suggestions), ensuring stable mappings across major RociError variants.
Add deep tests for utility modules RetryPolicy, ResponseCache, and UsageTracker covering correctness, time-dependent behavior, and concurrency edges.
Integrate rmcp child-process transport and replace stdio stub plumbing in MCP transport layer.

payload.description:
# ADR: CLI / core separation of concerns

## Goal
Capture the final SoC decision: `roci` core is UI-agnostic; `roci-cli` owns CLI UX; `roci-tools` owns built-in coding tools.

## Scope
- Create `docs/architecture/cli-soc.md` with:
  - Context/problem statement
  - Decision summary
  - Consequences (binary rename, tool path, error mapping)
  - Ownership boundaries per crate
- Add `read_when` entry in `docs/learned/LEARNINGS.md` pointing to this ADR.

## Acceptance criteria
1) ADR file exists at `docs/architecture/cli-soc.md`.
2) `docs/learned/LEARNINGS.md` updated with a `read_when` link.
3) ADR content reflects decisions in `tsq-jwv1ysxb.1`.

payload.text: Decision: 1B. On cancel/abort, do not persist partial assistant text; return prior stable state only. NOTE: This intentionally diverges from pi-mono, which materializes final assistant message even for aborted/error stops.
Decision: 2B. Perform breaking payload reshape for AgentEvent turn_end/agent_end parity target rather than additive-only fields. Include migration notes + test updates.
Decision: 3A. Retry defaults: max_attempts=3, base_delay_ms=250, factor=2.0, jitter=20%, max_delay_ms=2000; cancellation-aware sleep interruption required.
Decision: 4B. Overflow recovery detection uses typed provider error codes only (no free-text matcher fallback).

Decision: 5 custom precedence. API key resolution order: request override > provider/config key > runtime get_api_key callback. Rationale: preserve configured subscription auth before runtime/env overrides.
Decision: 6A with optional fields. MCP CLI uses repeatable structured flags; fields optional to support heterogenous server configs.

Decision update: align with pi-style cancellation materialization. Do not persist transient partial deltas, but do persist finalized assistant message returned by stream result on aborted/error path when available.

payload.text: Research summary: Roci already ships stdio + rmcp streamable HTTP client support (currently named SSETransport), MCPClient session bootstrap, multi-server tool aggregation, and instruction merging, but has no first-class WebSocket transport, client resource APIs, OAuth discovery/token lifecycle, or MCP server mode. Claude Code is the strongest prior art locally: it supports stdio/sse/http/ws client configs, resources/list + read, auth discovery/token caching, and reconnect heuristics, while its MCP server mode is stdio-only and still does not re-expose downstream MCP tools. Pi explicitly states No MCP. The local codex-cli checkout only contains a package/native launcher, so there is no inspectable MCP implementation source in this repo snapshot.

Assumptions + open questions: assume G5 HTTP + WS refers to client transports and that server mode v1 should expose Roci tools via server-core + stdio hosting only. Open questions to confirm before coding: should server mode later include SDK-hosted HTTP/WS serving; should the SDK ship a default browser/device-code helper or only callback traits; can SSETransport be renamed behind a compatibility alias now; and should aggregated resources use only (server_id, uri) or also publish a synthetic display id?

payload.text: Updated G5 after final product decision: MCP server mode v1 now includes stdio plus HTTP and WebSocket hosting. This increases implementation scope beyond earlier stdio-only guidance, so the server-mode plan now requires a shared server core with three thin host adapters rather than one stdio path.

New risks from HTTP/WS hosting scope: larger lifecycle surface area, more shutdown/backpressure/error-path cases, and a need to define host-boundary hooks for bind/listen/security without smuggling an opinionated production web stack into roci-core. Claude Code offers local prior art for stdio server mode only, so HTTP/WS serving will be designed with less direct competitor guidance.
Supersedes earlier planning note that assumed server mode was stdio-only: final product direction for G5 is stdio plus HTTP and WebSocket hosting in v1.

Resolved G1 implementation-start decisions: roci-core does not ship raw-text overflow fallback classifiers; roci-providers ships built-in text classifiers for first-party providers. Overflow recovery uses one output-budget reduction plus up to two compaction retries, and the second compaction retry only runs when the first materially changed context. Use a rich overflow taxonomy, exact-anchor plus estimated-tail context snapshots with confidence/source metadata, and structured compaction result metadata.

Resolved classifier boundary: roci-core consumes normalized overflow signals only; built-in raw-text overflow classifiers live in roci-providers. Use a rich taxonomy: input_overflow, output_overflow, request_too_large_bytes, silent_overflow, unknown_overflow.
Resolved recovery cap: one output-budget reduction plus up to two compaction retries. Only allow the second compaction retry when the first materially changed context state/size.

Resolved snapshot shape: ContextUsageSnapshot should use exact provider usage as anchor when available, add estimated tail usage, and expose confidence/source metadata instead of a pure heuristic-only view.
Resolved compaction result contract: include structured metadata such as pre/post token counts, preserved suffix/span info, and summary artifact metadata for tests/callers.
